title: hieracium fendleri subsp. fendleri
intro: Hieracium fendleri subsp. fendleri, also known as Fendler's hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The leaves and flowers of Fendler's hawkweed have been used in herbal preparations to promote digestive health and reduce fever, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Fendler's hawkweed may include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
